ops.title: TOON is not a default output format — only for uniform arrays of objects (≥100 rows) when it beats CSV/TSV by bytes; smart-format fallback is minified JSON

ops.body:
The user rejected TOON as the general fallback for LLM-bound JSON re-encoding: independent evals are contested and report TOON underperforming with Claude models specifically. In cc-context's `internal/format` classifier, TOON enters only its literature-clear-win domain — uniform arrays of objects, ≥100 rows (the only published floor), and it must strictly beat CSV/TSV on bytes in the shootout. The universal fallback is minified JSON, and every auto-mode pick obeys `len(out) ≤ len(compactJSON)`. Prose-dominant payloads unwrap to raw text + XML-ish metadata tags; markdown tables win under ~2K tokens (accuracy +7.6pp for +25–29% tokens); repeated nested shapes → TRON (gated on shape repeating ≥3×); heterogeneous → JSONL; YAML rejected (+21–27% tokens).

v0.5.1 tunings (user: "tune both"): (1) `candidateTolerance = 0.05` — encodeAuto picks the EARLIEST candidate within 5% of the smallest, so classifier ranking is a preference order and TOON now wins the null-bearing-table branch on near-ties (fidelity over a <5% markdown byte win); (2) `proseAbsoluteBytes = 2048` — any ≥2-word string field of 2 KiB+ fires prose regardless of the 0.66 share gate. The byte-net guard stays ABSOLUTE and outranks both: an assets-heavy `gh api` release object classifies prose but still emits compact JSON because prose tag overhead exceeds it — that's designed, don't "fix" it.

**Why:** the user corrected two plan drafts on this ("this is still missing the point; we just want smart formatting of JSON outputs"), supplying an 8-step decision chart and directing an independent literature pass.

**How to apply:** when recommending output formats for LLM context anywhere in the fleet, don't default to TOON; recommend `ccx format` / `BashFormat` (the classifier decides). Related: [[brew-first-installer]], [[toon-go-bigint-precision]].
